pageHero.label: Contact Us
pageHero.heading: Schedule Your Consultation Today
pageHero.subtext: Take the first step toward healthier, more beautiful skin. We're here to help.

contactInfo.heading: Get in Touch
contactInfo.description: Our friendly staff is ready to answer your questions and schedule your appointment. We look forward to welcoming you to DermaCare Clinic.
contactInfo.address: 123 Medical Plaza, Suite 200, Beverly Hills, CA 90210

questions.question: Do I need a referral to see a dermatologist?
questions.answer: Most insurance plans do not require a referral for dermatology visits, but we recommend checking with your insurance provider. We accept most major insurance plans and also welcome self-pay patients.

questions.question: What should I expect during my first visit?
questions.answer: Your first visit will include a comprehensive skin examination and discussion of your concerns. Please bring a list of current medications and any relevant medical records. The appointment typically lasts 30-45 minutes.

questions.question: How soon can I see results from cosmetic treatments?
questions.answer: Results vary by treatment. Botox takes 3-5 days to show effects with full results at two weeks. Fillers show immediate volume with final results after swelling subsides. Laser treatments and peels may require a series for optimal results.

questions.question: Are your treatments safe for all skin types?
questions.answer: Yes, our board-certified dermatologists are experienced in treating all skin types and tones. We customize every treatment plan to ensure safety and effectiveness for your unique skin.

questions.question: Do you offer payment plans?
questions.answer: We offer flexible payment options including CareCredit financing for cosmetic procedures. Medical dermatology services are typically covered by insurance. Our staff can discuss payment options during your consultation.

insurance.heading: Insurance & Payment
insurance.text: We accept most major insurance plans for medical dermatology services. Cosmetic procedures are typically not covered by insurance but can be financed through flexible payment options.
